name: La Source
notes: Tight hairpin immediately after the start/finish straight. First corner of the lap, major braking zone. Unchanged in 2022 revisions.

name: Eau Rouge
notes: Famous high-speed left-hander at the bottom of the valley, crossing the Eau Rouge stream. Taken flat in modern F1 cars. Unchanged in 2022 revisions.

name: Raidillon
notes: Steep uphill right-hander exiting Eau Rouge. Often grouped with Eau Rouge as 'Eau Rouge/Raidillon' but a distinct corner. A new asphalt runoff was added in the 2022 safety revisions but the corner itself is unchanged.

name: Kemmel Straight
notes: The long flat-out straight from Raidillon to Les Combes. Not a corner but a defining feature. Cars reach 320+ km/h. Listed here as the section entry point for image purposes.

name: Les Combes
notes: Right-left chicane at the top of the Kemmel hill. Primary overtaking opportunity on the circuit. Unchanged in 2022 revisions.

name: Malmedy
notes: Medium-speed right-hander after Les Combes. Named after the nearby Belgian town. Unchanged in 2022 revisions.

name: Rivage
notes: Slow hairpin right-hander, also called Bruxelles on some maps. Significant elevation change on exit. Unchanged in 2022 revisions.

name: Pouhon
notes: Very fast double-apex left-hander. One of the most demanding corners on the calendar at high speed. Unchanged in 2022 revisions.

name: Fagnes
notes: Right-hand corner in the Fagnes section, medium speed. Named after the Hautes Fagnes plateau region. Unchanged in 2022 revisions.

name: Campus
notes: Medium-speed right-hander. Name comes from the nearby Campus industrial zone. Unchanged in 2022 revisions.

name: Stavelot
notes: Right-hand corner leading onto the back section, named after the nearby town of Stavelot. Unchanged in 2022 revisions.

name: Paul Frère
notes: Fast left-right combination named after Belgian racing driver and journalist Paul Frère. Unchanged in 2022 revisions.

name: Blanchimont
notes: Very fast left-hander taken flat or near-flat in F1. Leads onto the final straight. Unchanged in 2022 revisions.

name: Bus Stop Chicane
notes: Final chicane before the start/finish straight. Left-right-left sequence. Heavy braking from high speed. Unchanged in 2022 revisions.
